file_name: Non Disclosure Agreement - CP Specific NDA (CreativeNDA-CPS121.1014).txt

perturbation.original_text: No P arty s hall make use of the other Party's name or any information acquired through its dealings with the other Party for publicity or marketing purposes without the prior writ ten consent of the other Party.
perturbation.changed_text: No P arty s hall make use of the other Party's name or any information acquired through its dealings with the other Party for publicity or marketing purposes without the prior written consent of the other Party, unless such use is required by law.
perturbation.explanation: By adding 'unless such use is required by law,' there's a potential conflict. While intended to allow legally mandated disclosures, it could be argued that any marketing activity can be justified under some legal interpretation, effectively nullifying the need for prior written consent. This contradicts the original intent of the clause which was to ensure absolute control over the use of one party's name by the other.
perturbation.contradicted_text: No P arty s hall make, or permit any person to make, any public announcement concerning this Agreement, the Purpose or its prospective interest in the Purpose without the prior written consent of the other Party (such consent not to be unreasonably withheld or delayed) except as required by law or any governmental or regulatory authority (including, without limitation, any relevant securities exchange) or by any C ourt or other authority of competent jurisdiction.
perturbation.justification: The Changed Text adds 'unless such use is required by law' to the restriction on using the other party's name, potentially nullifying the need for prior written consent. The Contradicted Text also contains the phrase 'except as required by law' in the context of public announcements. While both sections address exceptions for legal requirements, the Change in the Changed Text introduces ambiguity by potentially allowing broad interpretations of 'required by law,' particularly regarding marketing activities. This creates a direct conflict with the original intent of the first clause, which was to give absolute control over the use of names and information. Both clauses are now prone to conflicting interpretations.

perturbation.original_text: This A greement and any di spute or claim arising out of or in connection with it or its subject matter or formation (including non -contractual disputes or claims) shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the state of Delaware, of the United States of America, without regard to conflict of law rules thereof. The P arties irrevocably agree that the C ourts o f Delaware shall have exclusive jurisdiction to settle any dispute or claim that arises out of or in connection with this Agreement or its subject matter or formation (including non- contractual disputes or claims).
perturbation.changed_text: This A greement and any di spute or claim arising out of or in connection with it or its subject matter or formation (including non -contractual disputes or claims) shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the state of New York, of the United States of America, without regard to conflict of law rules thereof. The P arties irrevocably agree that the C ourts o f Delaware shall have exclusive jurisdiction to settle any dispute or claim that arises out of or in connection with this Agreement or its subject matter or formation (including non- contractual disputes or claims).
perturbation.explanation: This creates an in-text contradiction by stating that the agreement is governed by the laws of New York, while simultaneously stating that the courts of Delaware have exclusive jurisdiction. This leads to confusion regarding which state's laws will be applied in interpreting the agreement, creating a significant conflict.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This A greement and any di spute or claim arising out of or in connection with it or its subject matter or formation (including non -contractual disputes or claims) shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the state of New York, of the United States of America, without regard to conflict of law rules thereof. The P arties irrevocably agree that the C ourts o f Delaware shall have exclusive jurisdiction to settle any dispute or claim that arises out of or in connection with this Agreement or its subject matter or formation (including non- contractual disputes or claims).
perturbation.justification: The changed text alters the governing law from Delaware to New York, while simultaneously maintaining that Delaware courts have exclusive jurisdiction. This creates a direct and irreconcilable conflict because New York law would govern the interpretation of the agreement, yet any disputes would be settled in Delaware courts. The contradicted text repeats the new contradiction, reinforcing the conflict. This meets the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' because different sections of the contract (in this case, within the same section) provide conflicting obligations regarding the governing law.

perturbation.original_text: 4.1 All Conf idential Information shall remain the property of the Disclosing Party. Each Party reserves all rights in its Confidential Information. No rights, including, but not limited to, intellectual property rights, in respect of a party's Confidential Inform ation are granted to the other P arty and no obligations are imposed on the Disclosing Party other than those expressly stated in this A greement. Any background intellectual property r ights subsisting in a ny information provided by either Party to the other shall remain vested in the Disclosing Party and the Disclosing Party shall grant to the Recipient a non- exclusive, worldwide, royalty free, revocable licence to use such background Intellectual
perturbation.changed_text: 4.1 All Conf idential Information shall remain the property of the Disclosing Party, except for any improvements or derivatives made by the Recipient. Each Party reserves all rights in its Confidential Information. No rights, including, but not limited to, intellectual property rights, in respect of a party's Confidential Inform ation are granted to the other P arty and no obligations are imposed on the Disclosing Party other than those expressly stated in this A greement. Any background intellectual property r ights subsisting in a ny information provided by either Party to the other shall remain vested in the Disclosing Party and the Disclosing Party shall grant to the Recipient a non- exclusive, worldwide, royalty free, revocable licence to use such background Intellectual
perturbation.explanation: The amendment stating that the Recipient owns 'improvements or derivatives' of the Disclosing Party's confidential information directly contradicts the original clause which asserts all Confidential Information remains the property of the Disclosing Party. This creates significant uncertainty about ownership of modifications or new creations based on the initial confidential information, leading to conflicts over intellectual property rights.
perturbation.contradicted_text: All Conf idential Information shall remain the property of the Disclosing Party, except for any improvements or derivatives made by the Recipient. Each Party reserves all rights in its Confidential Information. No rights, including, but not limited to, intellectual property rights, in respect of a party's Confidential Inform ation are granted to the other P arty and no obligations are imposed on the Disclosing Party other than those expressly stated in this A greement. Any background intellectual property r ights subsisting in a ny information provided by either Party to the other shall remain vested in the Disclosing Party and the Disclosing Party shall grant to the Recipient a non- exclusive, worldwide, royalty free, revocable licence to use such background Intellectual
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies section 4.1 to state that the Recipient owns 'improvements or derivatives' of the Disclosing Party's confidential information. This directly contradicts the initial assertion in the same section, which declares that all Confidential Information remains the property of the Disclosing Party. The contradictory text is located in the same section, thus it constitutes a strong in-text contradiction as per the 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' definition.

perturbation.original_text: Except as otherwise pr ovided in this Agreement, no Party may assign, sub- contract or deal in any way with, any of its rights or obligations under this Agreement or any document referred to in it.
perturbation.changed_text: Except as otherwise pr ovided in this Agreement, no Party may assign, sub- contract or deal in any way with, any of its rights or obligations under this Agreement or any document referred to in it, except to its Affiliates.
perturbation.explanation: Adding 'except to its Affiliates' contradicts the general prohibition on assignment. While seemingly permissive, it creates uncertainty because 'Affiliates' isn't precisely defined here (and a conflicting definition is not being created). Other clauses might implicitly assume that assignment requires consent, and this addition undermines that assumption by potentially allowing unilateral assignment to an ambiguous group. This creates a loophole and ambiguity in enforcement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Recipi ent shall keep the Disclosing Party's Confidential Information confidential and, except with the prior written consent of the Disclosing Part y, shall: (a) not use or ex ploit the Confidential Information in any way except for the Purpose; (b) not disclose or make available the Confidential Information in whole or in part to any third party, except as expressly permitted by this A greement; (c) not copy, reduce to writing or otherwise record the Confidential Information except as strictlynecessary for the Purpose (and any such copies, reductions to writing and records shall be theproperty of the Disclosing Party); (d) keep separate the Confidential Information from all documents and other records of theRecipient; (e) apply the same security measures and degree of care to the Confidential Information as theRecipient applies to its own confidential information, which the Recipient warrants as providingadequate p rotection from unauthorised disclosure, copying or use; and (f) keep a written record of: any document or other Confidential Information received from the otherin tangible form; any copy made of the Confidential Information.
perturbation.justification: The original text prohibited any assignment. The changed text now allows assignment to 'Affiliates.' The contradicted text requires prior written consent for disclosing confidential information to any third party. The term 'Affiliates' is not precisely defined, so it's unclear if they qualify as a 'third party' requiring consent. This creates a contradiction because the assignment clause potentially allows disclosure of confidential information to affiliates without the prior written consent mandated by the contradicted text. This fits the 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' definition as it introduces a conflicting obligation by potentially circumventing the confidentiality requirements.
